payload.text:
Build a modern, professional, enterprise-grade, fully responsive website for:
Progressive Health Initiative of Liberia (PHIL Inc.)
PHIL Inc. is a registered not-for-profit health NGO dedicated to advancing health equity, empowering communities, and improving access to healthcare services across Liberia and globally.
The website must communicate:
Trust
Transparency
Global NGO credibility
Donor confidence
Community impact
Professional legitimacy
Partnership readiness
The design must meet standards comparable to:
WHO
UNICEF partner NGOs
Global health NGOs
International nonprofit organizations
________________________________________
OVERALL DESIGN STYLE
Theme:
Light primary theme with optional dark mode toggle
Primary colors:
Medical Blue (#0A84FF)
Health Teal (#00C2A8)
Clean White (#FFFFFF)
Accent colors:
Soft Green (#22C55E)
Professional Navy (#0F172A)
Design characteristics:
Clean
Modern
Professional
Humanitarian NGO feel
Enterprise-grade credibility
Typography:
Primary font: Inter
Secondary font: Poppins or Source Sans Pro
Layout requirements:
Fully responsive
Mobile-first
Accessible (WCAG compliant)
Clean spacing
Professional hierarchy
Animations:
Smooth scroll
Fade-in on scroll
Subtle hover elevation
Professional transitions
Avoid flashy or startup-style visuals.
________________________________________
HERO SECTION
Include:
Organization Name:
Progressive Health Initiative of Liberia (PHIL Inc.)
Motto:
HEALTH IS LIFE!
Headline:
Advancing Health Equity and Empowering Communities Across Liberia
Supporting Statement:
PHIL Inc. is a registered nonprofit health organization committed to improving health outcomes through education, advocacy, community outreach, and sustainable health programs for underserved populations.
CTA Buttons:
Donate Now
Become a Volunteer
Partner With Us
Learn About Our Programs
Include trust indicators:
Founded in 2022
Community-based NGO
Serving underserved populations
Health equity focused
Hero image:
Real community outreach or health program image.
________________________________________
ABOUT SECTION
Include:
Full About description
Mission Statement
Vision Statement
Core Values:
Equity
Integrity
Collaboration
Empowerment
Innovation
Compassion
Display values using icon cards.
________________________________________
PROGRAMS & THEMATIC AREAS SECTION
Create professional cards for:
Public Health Promotion
Mental Health & Psychosocial Support
Water, Sanitation & Hygiene (WASH)
Sexual and Reproductive Health
Maternal and Child Health
Nutrition
Youth Empowerment and Education
Emergency & Humanitarian Health Response
Each card includes:
Icon
Title
Description
Learn More button
Glassmorphism card design.
________________________________________
IMPACT & ACHIEVEMENTS SECTION
Display PHIL Inc. achievements:
Community Health Seminar (Sept 2024)
Improve Health, Save Lives Project (March 2025)
Community Health Radio Program
Environmental Health Initiatives
Online Health Certificate Programs
Include animated counters:
Communities reached
Programs conducted
People impacted
Volunteers engaged
________________________________________
TARGET POPULATION SECTION
Display target beneficiaries:
Rural and underserved communities
Women and children
Youth and adolescents
Persons with disabilities
Vulnerable populations
________________________________________
LEADERSHIP SECTION
CEO Profile:
Name:
Isaac Mendoabor Bleh, Jr.
Title:
Founder & Executive Director
Summary:
Public health advocate and community development leader dedicated to improving health outcomes and empowering underserved communities through education, advocacy, and sustainable health programs.
Include photo and leadership credentials.
________________________________________
PARTNERS SECTION
Display collaboration categories:
Government agencies
International organizations
Universities
NGOs
Community organizations
Logo grid layout.
________________________________________
GET INVOLVED SECTION
Include three cards:
Volunteer
Join our volunteer network
Donate
Support our health programs
Partner With Us
Collaborate with PHIL Inc.
________________________________________
DONATION SECTION (OPEN AMOUNT + MOBILE MONEY + BANK TRANSFER)
This must be a major standalone section.
________________________________________
Donation Header
Headline:
Support Health Equity and Save Lives
Supporting text:
Your donation supports health programs, outreach initiatives, and healthcare access for underserved communities.
________________________________________
Open Donation Input
Field:
Enter donation amount (any amount)
Currency:
USD
LRD
Button:
Donate
________________________________________
Mobile Money Donation
Title:
Donate via Mobile Money
Display:
Mobile Network Name
Account Name: Progressive Health Initiative of Liberia (PHIL Inc.)
Mobile Number: [INSERT OFFICIAL NUMBER]
Button:
I Have Sent My Donation
________________________________________
Bank Transfer Donation
Display:
Bank Name: [INSERT BANK NAME]
Account Name: Progressive Health Initiative of Liberia (PHIL Inc.)
Account Number: [INSERT ACCOUNT NUMBER]
SWIFT Code (optional)
Include copy buttons.
________________________________________
Donation Confirmation Form
Fields:
Full Name
Email
Phone
Amount
Payment Method
Transaction Reference
Button:
Submit Donation Confirmation
________________________________________
Trust Indicators
Transparent nonprofit
Community-focused
Accountable use of funds
________________________________________
TESTIMONIALS SECTION
Critical trust-building section.
Header:
What People Say About PHIL Inc.
Display testimonial cards with:
Photo
Name
Role
Testimonial text
Use animated slider.
Testimonial types:
Community members
Volunteers
Partners
Beneficiaries
Optional video testimonials.
________________________________________
NEWS & UPDATES SECTION
Display:
Events
Programs
Announcements
Card layout.
________________________________________
CONTACT SECTION
Include:
Contact form:
Name
Email
Message
Contact info:
Email
Phone
Location
Google Map embed.
________________________________________
NAVIGATION MENU
Include:
Home
About
Programs
Impact
Leadership
Donate
Testimonials
Contact
________________________________________
FOOTER
Include:
Organization name
Quick links
Contact info
Social media
Copyright:
© 2026 Progressive Health Initiative of Liberia (PHIL Inc.)
________________________________________
STICKY DONATE BUTTON
Floating donate button visible at all times.
________________________________________
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
HTML5 semantic structure
Modern CSS (Grid, Flexbox)
Minimal JavaScript
Fast loading
Mobile optimized
SEO optimized
________________________________________
CONVERSION GOALS
Optimize website for:
Donors
Volunteers
Partners
International NGOs
Community members
________________________________________
DESIGN GOAL
The website must feel like:
A trusted international health NGO
Professional
Transparent
Legitimate
Global-standard
NOT basic
NOT personal
NOT startup-style
Enterprise nonprofit quality.

**Defining Project Scope**

Okay, I'm focusing on scoping the project for PHIL Inc. I'm thinking about a professional, enterprise-grade React website, keeping in mind they're a not-for-profit health NGO. The goal is to define their online presence.


**Visualizing Key Components**

I'm now zeroing in on the visual identity and site components. I've selected a primary color palette focused on Medical Blue and Health Teal, with Clean White for backgrounds. I will use Soft Green and Professional Navy as accents. The design style will be clean, modern, professional, humanitarian, and enterprise-grade. I am considering Inter, Poppins, and Source Sans Pro for the typography. Finally, I've outlined the core page sections and technologies.


**Detailing Core Implementation**

I'm now detailing the core implementation. I'm choosing Lucide React for icons and Framer Motion for professional animations, like fade-ins and smooth scrolls. I plan to build a custom slider for testimonials using Framer Motion. I am going mobile-first, so that will be a priority. I have a rough file/folder structure outlined for the website. Finally, I have some section goals in place.

payload.text: aminate the images and make them engaging with modern amination

**Assessing image animation needs**

I'm currently reviewing the `App.tsx` file for PHIL Inc.'s website. I've pinpointed key image locations for potential animation: the hero section background, the "about" section image, and the leadership (CEO) section image. My next step involves determining the specific animation style best suited for each.


**Expanding Animation Scope**

I've expanded the animation considerations to include news section images, testimonial images, and various decorative icons. This necessitates a more comprehensive design specification. For the hero image, I'm leaning toward a subtle zoom or parallax. About section will be a fade-in with slide or "floating" effect, leadership image reveal or hover zoom. News cards get a hover scale-up with shadow. Icons get fade-up entrance animations. Globally, I want to use Intersection Observer for scroll-based reveals.


**Implementing Animation Framework**

I've got the keyframes in mind and will add them to `index.html`. My focus is shifting to `App.tsx`, where I'll define classes to trigger the animation keyframes. I'm exploring using a `reveal` state or utilizing hover and group-hover states to achieve interactive effects, all while considering image overlays and borders for visual enhancement. A detailed plan has emerged for specific animation effects in the hero, about, programs, and leadership sections.

About the CEO of Progressive Health Initiative Liberia. 
Name: Isaac Mendoabor Bleh, Jr.
Founder & Executive Director, Progressive Health Initiative of Liberia (PHIL Inc.)
Isaac Mendoabor Bleh, Jr. is a public health advocate, community development practitioner, and emerging global health leader committed to improving health outcomes in marginalized and underserved communities across Liberia.
He is currently studying Biology at William V.S. Tubman University and pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Public Health at the University of the People (USA – online). Isaac is a graduate of the Aspire Leaders Program (Harvard Business School, USA) and holds a Diploma in Global Leadership and Strategic Management from the 100 Million Learners Institute. He has also completed professional development programs with African Mind Builders and other leadership and capacity-building institutions.
Isaac has extensive experience in health promotion, community advocacy, youth leadership, and social development. He serves as a Volunteer Health Advocate at PHIL Inc., a Rural Advocacy and Education Officer (RAEO) with Street Child International, and an Officer Coordinator for Mission Support Ministry International. He is also the President of the Liberian Science Students Association (William V.S. Tubman University Chapter) and President-Elect of Edu-Think Africa, Class of 2025.
Through his work, Isaac has contributed to health education campaigns, community outreach initiatives, rural advocacy programs, youth empowerment activities, and public health awareness efforts, with particular focus on preventive health, water and sanitation, maternal and child health, community health education, and behavior change communication. He has been actively involved in engaging communities through outreach programs, trainings, and health-focused media platforms.
Isaac founded the Progressive Health Initiative of Liberia (PHIL Inc.) in response to the persistent health challenges facing marginalized populations, especially in rural and underserved environments. His vision is to strengthen community knowledge, promote preventive healthcare, and support sustainable health interventions that empower individuals and communities to take ownership of their well-being.
Guided by the values of equity, service, integrity, and community empowerment, Isaac is passionate about building partnerships, developing young leaders, and advancing innovative, community-driven solutions for improved public health in Liberia and beyond.
